title: persona rotate-key CLI command — new RSA keypair + Update(Person) delivery
description:
## What
Implement `smallhold persona rotate-key <username>` CLI subcommand. Generate a new RSA-2048 keypair via OsRng. Update the accounts row atomically. Send an Update(Person) activity to all followers announcing the new public key. Verify the new key passes `openssl rsa -check`.

## Why
Key rotation is a security hygiene requirement. Without it, a compromised private key cannot be safely replaced without losing federation. Part of sub-epic 8-A polish.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read the ActivityPub Update(Person) activity shape from the spec and from mastodon.social live Update actor fixture (mastodon.social broadcasts these when profile changes); check activitypub-federation crate docs for how it handles Update Actor activities; document the exact JSON shape required for the publicKey embedded in the actor document.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add `persona rotate-key <username>` to the CLI (clap derive); generate RSA-2048 keypair with `rsa` crate + OsRng; serialize private key as PKCS#8 PEM and public key as SPKI PEM; update accounts row in a single transaction (set new private_key_pem, public_key_pem); load all follower inboxes from followers table; enqueue one Update(Person) activity per unique shared inbox (or per follower inbox if no shared inbox); deliver via the existing outbound delivery worker.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write a test that calls the rotate-key path, then verifies the new private key parses correctly with the `rsa` crate; verifies the Update(Person) activity JSON has the correct @context, type=Update, object.type=Person, object.publicKey.publicKeyPem field; verifies the activity is delivered to the test follower inbox URL (use a mock HTTP server like `wiremock`).

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: ActivityPub spec Section 7.3 (Update activity); mastodon.social live Update(Person) fixture captured via curl; `openssl rsa -check -in new_key.pem` exit code 0 is the key validity oracle.
- Test approach: integration test; generate a keypair, verify with openssl subprocess call in test; verify Update activity JSON shape against mastodon.social fixture (not against the serialization code itself); use `wiremock` for the inbox delivery assertion.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Generate key with OsRng (not a seeded RNG)
- Perform DB update and delivery enqueue in same transaction — do not update DB without enqueuing
- Do not log the private key at any log level
- Verify the new keypair round-trips before committing to DB (sign + verify a test message)
- No `.unwrap()` anywhere in the CLI path
- If username does not exist, exit with non-zero code and clear error message
